text: The surgeon incises the peritoneum and creates a preperitoneal flap proximal to the medial aspect of the hernia defect. The hernia sac is reduced, revealing the posterior aspect of the defect, which is then closed primarily using OV-lock suture.

The surgeon reduces the pneumoperitoneum to eight millimeters of mercury and completes the re-approximation of the transversus and internal oblique muscles at the posterior aspect of the hernia defect. A 20x25 cm sheet of physio mesh is placed over the repair and secured to the abdominal wall using secure strap tackers. The peritoneal flap is re-approximated with tackers, ensuring no mesh is exposed.
